summary: Parallel review: FAIL (2 critical, 8 warning, 8 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:288` — The "reverts uncommitted changes" test captures only `checkout HEAD` commands; `git clean -fd` (simplify-pass.mjs:189–193) is never asserted and can be silently deleted without failing any test. Carried from Tester/PM/Architect run_3 — assign to backlog.

🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:1578` — `phaseOrder` hardcodes `["brainstorm", "build", "review"]` at both line 1578 (console breakdown) and line 1604 (progress log); `"simplify"` absent despite `setUsageContext("simplify", null)` at line 1506. Carried through 4+ review passes — assign to backlog.

The two 🟡 findings are pre-existing backlog items carried from task-1 review passes — neither is introduced or worsened by task-2. No new architectural concerns. The implementation of `git merge-base HEAD main → ${base}..HEAD` is architecturally correct for full-branch diff.

🟡 `test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:294` — `git clean -fd` at `simplify-pass.mjs:188` is never captured or asserted in the "reverts uncommitted changes" test; removing that revert call leaves all 468 tests green — add `if (cmd.includes("clean")) revertCmds.push(cmd)` to the mock and `assert.ok(revertCmds.some(c => c.includes("clean")))` to lock in the untracked-file cleanup fix

The core feature claim — that the simplify pass uses `git merge-base HEAD main` (falling back to `master`) to get a full branch-diff scope, not a per-task diff — is directly verified by three new behavioral tests (lines 355–427). Builder claims for both tasks confirmed against source. One 🟡 carried from task-1 review cycle remains unresolved: the `git clean -fd` fix exists in code but the test cannot detect its removal.

🟡 `bin/lib/simplify-pass.mjs:47` — `base` (from `git merge-base` output) is interpolated directly into a shell command string. `execSync` with a string argument runs via `/bin/sh -c`, making shell metacharacters in `base` executable. In practice `base` is always a hex SHA, but `getChangedFiles` is a named export — add `/^[0-9a-f]{7,40}$/i` validation before interpolation to harden the API surface.
🟡 `bin/lib/simplify-pass.mjs:178` — `preSha` interpolated into `git reset --hard ${preSha}` with no format check. Same fix: validate SHA format before use.
No critical (🔴) findings. No warning (🟡) findings introduced by this task.
| Dead code | PASS | Prior 🔴 (`let revParseCount = 0;`) absent from lines 154–176. All new test variables (`capturedDiffCmd`, `cmds`, `dispatches`) are read and used. |
